Bug Review · Multi-Agent Static Audit

LIMO-EEG Toolbox — Verified Bug Report

Two Opus 4.8 multi-agent passes over the current stable release v4.2.1 (synced with origin/v4.2.1): a 333-agent round 1, then a round 2 seeded with MATLAB checkcode and told to find only bugs the first pass missed. Every candidate was re-checked by an independent adversarial verifier that had to construct a concrete failing input against the real code or reject it.

218 from round 169 new in round 2 Opus 4.8 · adversarially verified 87 files implicated

Critical 4 High 83 Medium 123 Low 77 269 CONFIRMED · 18 PLAUSIBLE
How to read this. Findings are deduplicated (dual-lens + cross-cutting overlaps merged by file&line) and ranked by severity, then verdict. CONFIRMED means a verifier built a concrete reproduction against the real code; PLAUSIBLE means a likely defect that needs runtime/data to fully settle. The 4 round-1 criticals were reproduced in MATLAB R2025a and fixed (PRs #234–#237); round 2 added 69 distinct findings (0 duplicates of round 1) whose verification was partially cut short by a session limit, so that count is a floor. Use the round filter to isolate them. Scope note: external/ (FieldTrip, PSOM, apcluster) and deprecated/ were intentionally excluded as third-party / retired code.

Generated by a multi-agent workflow (find → independent adversarial verify) over LIMO-EEG v4.2.1. Line numbers reference the audited working tree. Fixes are minimal suggestions, not applied changes.
